sections.paragraph:
Κυρηναῖος, ου, ὁ (s. next entry; Hdt. et al.; OGI 767, 31; Michel 897, 26; PPetr I, 16 [1], 3; 22 [1], 3; 2 Macc 2:23; Joseph.) a Cyrenian (s. Κυρήνη) with the article Ac 13:1 (Socrates, Ep. 28, 1 Θεόδωρος ὁ Κ.; Athen. 7, 14 p. 281c). Without the article Mk 15:21; Lk 23:26 (Diod S 11, 84, 1 Πολύμναστος Κυρηναῖος).—Adj. ἄνθρωπος Κ. Mt 27:32. ἄνδρες Κ. Ac 11:20. In Jerusalem the Cyrenian Jews had a synagogue, either for themselves alone, or together w. other Jews of the Diaspora 6:9 (Schürer II 428; III 60f).—BZimolong, BZ 21, ’33, 184–88; EBishop, ET 51, ’39/40, 148–53; WBarclay, ET 72, ’60, 28–31.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Michel Michel = CM., Recueil d’inscriptions grecques—List 3

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

BZ BZ = Biblische Zeitschrift—List 6

ET ET = Expository Times—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 575
